summary: Finite page navigation with shared item-window generation, ellipsis rendering, current-page semantics, and React/Vue/Svelte adapters.
philosophy: Keep page-window generation in core so every adapter renders the same pages and ellipses.
Use a labelled nav with list semantics so pagination is discoverable as page navigation.
Keep visual dimensions in firstEdition CSS to preserve Figma fidelity without adapter-specific styles.
Expose a compact controlled API in every framework while supporting uncontrolled starting state where idiomatic.

useWhen: A finite collection is split across known pages.
Users need direct page jumps in addition to previous and next controls.
The interface should show a compact page range with ellipses.
avoidWhen: Content loads continuously — use infinite scroll or load more.
Pages represent steps in a task flow — use a step navigator.
The selection controls visible panel content — use tabs.

automationCovers: Core recipe marks the current page with aria-current.
Adapters disable previous and next controls at page boundaries.
React, Vue, and Svelte render labelled navigation and page buttons.
Storybook taxonomy tests across React, Vue, and Svelte.
manualReviewRequired: Assistive-technology announcement of page changes in consuming product contexts.
Keyboard focus order with surrounding data table controls.
High-contrast appearance of selected and focused page items.
notes: Initial implementation uses explicit page buttons and hidden ellipsis text for assistive technology.
Page-change announcements can be added by consuming views when content updates asynchronously.

openQuestions: Should a compact icon-only previous/next variant be added later?
Should async page loading expose a busy/loading state on the root?
